text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST
PROTOCOL-SPECIFIED ON-TRIAL RESTAGING STUDY

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Yuen, 65-year-old female with known Stage IV (M1b) non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma histology, right upper lobe primary, EGFR L858R-mutant, MET-amplified. Currently enrolled in Phase II investigator-sponsored clinical trial of savolitinib 300 mg once daily plus Tagrisso 80 mg once daily for MET-amplified EGFR-mutant NSCLC following progression on third-generation EGFR TKI. History of prior stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) to three intracranial metastases (left frontal lobe 12 mm, right cerebellar hemisphere 8 mm, left parietal lobe 6 mm). Presenting for 6-month protocol-specified restaging evaluation. Prior on-trial 8-week imaging demonstrated partial response with right upper lobe mass measuring 1.4 cm and favorable CNS response. Comparison is made to the 8-week on-trial CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain.


CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following administration of 90 mL of iohexol (Omnipaque 350) intravenous contrast. Imaging was acquired in the portal venous phase. Axial images reconstructed at 2.5 mm intervals with coronal and sagittal reformats. Lung parenchyma evaluated with dedicated lung windowing. Comparison: Prior on-trial 8-week CT (right upper lobe mass 1.4 cm) and baseline diagnostic CT at time of diagnosis (right upper lobe mass 5.8 x 4.2 cm).

FINDINGS:

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:
The right upper lobe contains a residual irregular, spiculated mass with internal architectural distortion measuring 1.3 cm in greatest transaxial diameter (previously 1.4 cm at 8-week on-trial scan, and 2.7 cm at time of documented systemic progression, and 5.8 cm at initial diagnosis). The lesion demonstrates mild internal heterogeneity with peripheral ground-glass attenuation consistent with a treatment-related halo effect or residual peri-tumoral fibrotic change. Pleural tagging and linear fibrotic stranding extend from the mass toward the adjacent visceral pleural surface, unchanged from prior imaging and consistent with post-treatment fibrotic sequelae. There is no convincing satellite nodularity adjacent to this lesion. No new pulmonary nodules or masses are identified in the right upper, middle, or lower lobes. The left lung is clear. No consolidation or ground-glass opacities suspicious for infection or drug-related pneumonitis in either lung. The central airways including the trachea, bilateral mainstem bronchi, and lobar bronchi are patent without endobronchial lesion. No new pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
No pathologically enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes are identified. The previously described right paratracheal (4R) lymph node, which measured 1.1 cm at time of systemic progression and subsequently decreased to 0.7 cm after bridging chemotherapy, is no longer discretely visualized as a distinct nodal structure; there is a small residual soft tissue focus in this station measuring approximately 6 mm in short axis, likely representing a normal-sized reactive node or post-treatment fibrous residuum. No enlarged right hilar, subcarinal, aortopulmonary window, or prevascular lymph nodes. Left paratracheal station and left hilar nodes are within normal limits. The mediastinal fat planes are preserved. The esophagus is unremarkable. No pericardial effusion. The heart is normal in size.

CHEST WALL AND BONY THORAX:
No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions in the ribs, thoracic vertebral bodies, scapulae, clavicles, or sternum. Post-treatment fibrotic pleural thickening is noted along the right apex in the region of prior chest wall abutment, stable in appearance. No rib erosion or pathological fracture.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
LIVER: Normal in size. Hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesion. No hypodense or hypervascular masses identified. No hepatic metastases. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein is patent.
GALLBLADDER: Unremarkable. No cholelithiasis.
PANCREAS: Pancreatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal mass or ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding.
SPLEEN: Normal in size and homogeneous attenuation.
ADRENAL GLANDS: Both adrenal glands are visualized and unremarkable. No adrenal nodule or mass is present bilaterally. No new adrenal lesion. Incidental note is made of minimal left adrenal limb thickening measuring 5 mm, unchanged from prior examinations and below the threshold for concern.
KIDNEYS: Both kidneys are normal in size with symmetric enhancement and excretion of contrast. No renal mass, cyst complex in appearance, or hydronephrosis. No perirenal infiltration.
STOMACH AND BOWEL: Visualized loops of small bowel and large bowel are normal in caliber without wall thickening, mesenteric stranding, or obstruction. Stomach is unremarkable.
PERITONEUM AND RETROPERITONEUM: No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or implants. No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes. Aorta is normal in caliber.

PELVIS:
Uterus and ovaries are visualized; uterus appears atrophic, consistent with postmenopausal status. No adnexal masses. No pelvic sidewall adenopathy. No free fluid in the pelvis. Urinary bladder is decompressed; no filling defect. Osseous structures of the pelvis show no suspicious lesions.


MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE:
MRI of the brain was performed on a 3 Tesla scanner using a dedicated brain coil. Sequences obtained: sagittal T1, axial T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with ADC mapping, axial gradient echo susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI), and axial and coronal T1 fat-saturated post-gadolinium sequences following administration of 0.1 mmol/kg gadobutrol (Gadavist) intravenous contrast. Comparison: Most recent MRI brain from 8-week on-trial restaging (left frontal lesion 4 mm, right cerebellar lesion 4 mm, left parietal lesion not visualized). Baseline MRI at SRS planning: left frontal 12 mm, right cerebellar 8 mm, left parietal 6 mm.

FINDINGS:

KNOWN METASTATIC SITES:

1. Left Frontal Lobe (previously 12 mm at SRS planning, 7 mm post-SRS/pre-trial, 4 mm at 8-week on-trial scan): There is a punctate focus of T1 post-contrast enhancement in the left frontal white matter measuring approximately 2 mm in greatest dimension. This represents significant interval reduction from the 4 mm measurement on the 8-week on-trial scan. Surrounding T2/FLAIR hyperintensity has completely resolved. No restricted diffusion. No susceptibility blooming to suggest hemorrhagic transformation. The adjacent cortex and overlying sulcal architecture are intact. This near-complete resolution is consistent with favorable treatment response to combined SRS and savolitinib/osi systemic therapy. The residual 2 mm focus may represent a small treatment scar, residual pial vessel, or minimally viable tumor and will require continued close follow-up.

2. Right Cerebellar Hemisphere (previously 8 mm at SRS planning, 6 mm post-SRS, 4 mm at 8-week on-trial scan): There is a small focus of T1 post-contrast enhancement in the right cerebellar hemisphere measuring 4 mm, essentially unchanged from the 8-week on-trial scan. There is no surrounding T2/FLAIR signal abnormality. No mass effect on the fourth ventricle. No restricted diffusion. No significant susceptibility artifact to suggest radiation necrosis with hemorrhagic component. Appearances are most consistent with stable post-SRS change versus small stable metastatic residuum. Given unchanged size over the interval period and favorable overall clinical trajectory, this is interpreted as stable disease.

3. Left Parietal Lobe (previously 6 mm at SRS planning, 3 mm post-SRS/pre-trial, not visualized at 8-week on-trial scan): No enhancing lesion is identified in the left parietal lobe on current examination. The prior SRS treatment site shows no residual enhancement, consistent with prior complete local response. No T2/FLAIR signal abnormality at this location. No new parietal lobe lesion.

GENERAL BRAIN PARENCHYMA:
No new enhancing lesions identified anywhere in the supratentorial or infratentorial brain on post-contrast sequences. No new parenchymal masses, hemorrhage, or areas of abnormal diffusion restriction suggestive of new ischemic injury or new metastatic deposits. The cortex, deep gray nuclei, and cerebellum are intact. Ventricular system is normal in size and configuration without hydrocephalus. No midline shift. Sulci are appropriate in caliber for the patient's age.

LEPTOMENINGEAL DISEASE:
No leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal enhancement on post-contrast sequences. Basilar cisterns are patent. No cranial nerve enhancement. No ependymal enhancement.

OTHER FINDINGS:
The previously described scattered T2/FLAIR periventricular and subcortical white matter hyperintensities are stable and consistent with chronic small vessel ischemic changes, unchanged from prior examinations and of no new clinical significance. Mild volume loss is present in a pattern consistent with age-appropriate involutional change. Major intracranial vessels appear grossly patent on flow-sensitive sequences. Orbits, paranasal sinuses, and skull base are unremarkable on sequences obtained.


IMPRESSION:

1. RIGHT UPPER LOBE RESIDUAL MASS: The right upper lobe spiculated residual mass measures 1.3 cm, essentially stable compared with the 8-week on-trial measurement of 1.4 cm. This represents an overall marked reduction from the 5.8 cm mass documented at initial diagnosis and the 2.7 cm mass at time of documented systemic Tagrisso progression. The lesion is markedly diminished and has been stable over the most recent interval, consistent with ongoing partial response or plateau of treatment effect. No new intrathoracic lesions.

2. MEDIASTINAL AND HILAR ADENOPATHY: No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. The previously enlarged right paratracheal node has diminished to a subcentimeter residual soft tissue focus, consistent with near-complete nodal response.

3. INTRACRANIAL DISEASE: Favorable intracranial response is maintained. The left frontal lesion has nearly completely resolved (now 2 mm from prior 4 mm). The right cerebellar lesion is stable at 4 mm, consistent with post-SRS residual. The left parietal treatment site remains clear. No new intracranial metastases. No leptomeningeal disease.

4. NO EVIDENCE OF NEW DISTANT METASTATIC DISEASE in the liver, adrenal glands, kidneys, peritoneum, pelvis, or visualized osseous structures.

5. Overall findings are consistent with continued partial response and stable disease at the 6-month on-trial assessment for savolitinib plus osi combination therapy. Compared with imaging at the time of systemic progression from Tagrisso monotherapy, there has been substantial reduction in the right upper lobe primary and complete or near-complete resolution of all previously documented sites of nodal and intracranial involvement.
